questions.text: Down’s cell is used to prepare:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sodium carbonate is not extracted in Down's cell; it is typically manufactured using the Solvay process.
Sodium bicarbonate is a compound that is not produced in Down's cell; it is usually obtained by reacting sodium carbonate with carbon dioxide and water.
Sodium hydroxide is produced through the chlor-alkali process, not Down's cell, which is specifically used for extracting sodium metal.
Down's cell is designed to extract sodium metal through the electrolysis of molten sodium chloride, where sodium ions are reduced at the cathode to form sodium metal.

questions.explanation: Down's cell is a method for obtaining sodium metal by the electrolysis of molten sodium chloride. In this process, graphite serves as the anode, releasing chlorine gas, while a steel cathode collects the molten sodium metal. Sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, and sodium hydroxide are not products of this process. Sodium carbonate is produced using the Solvay process, sodium bicarbonate is derived from sodium carbonate, and sodium hydroxide is produced through the chlor-alkali process.

questions.text: Which one of the following is larger in size than the oxygen atom?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Fluorine has a smaller atomic size than oxygen because it is to the right of oxygen in the periodic table. As you move across a period from left to right, the nuclear charge increases, pulling the electrons closer to the nucleus and reducing the atomic radius. Fluorine has an atomic radius of 42 pm, which is smaller than oxygen’s 48 pm, making it an incorrect option.

Nitrogen is incorrect because, although it is larger than oxygen, it is not the only correct answer. Carbon is also larger than oxygen, and based on general periodic trends, carbon is even larger than nitrogen.

Carbon is even further to the left of oxygen in the periodic table, meaning it has a lower nuclear charge and a weaker attraction between the nucleus and the outermost electrons. This results in a larger atomic radius of 70 pm, which is significantly larger than oxygen’s 48 pm. Carbon is a correct option.
Hydrogen (H) is in the first period and has only one electron, making it one of the smallest atoms in the periodic table, much smaller than the oxygen atom, which has two electron shells.

questions.explanation: Atomic size generally decreases across a period from left to right due to increasing nuclear charge. Oxygen is to the right of carbon in the periodic table, so it is smaller. Therefore, carbon, being to the left of oxygen, is larger in size.

questions.text: The general name of the ore CaSO4.1/2H2O is _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The formula of gypsum is CaSO4.2H2O.
The formula of dolomite is CaMg(CO3)2
The formula of calcite is : CaCO3
The formula of plaster of Paris is: Ca SO4.1/2H2O.

questions.explanation: CaSO4.1/2H2O is also known as Plaster of Paris.

questions.text: Which one of the following alkali metals forms only normal oxide when it reacts with O2?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Only Lithium burns in the air to form a normal oxide Li2O (white solid). Sodium, Potassium, and Rubidium form both normal oxide and peroxide when they react with oxygen.Li + O2 --->Li2O (Lithium oxide)
Sodium, Potassium, and Rubidium form both normal oxide and peroxide when they react with oxygen.
Sodium, Potassium, and Rubidium form both normal oxide and peroxide when they react with oxygen.
Sodium, Potassium, and Rubidium form both normal oxide and peroxide when they react with oxygen.

questions.explanation: Only Lithium forms a normal oxide Li2O when it reacts with oxygen. This is because Lithium is less reactive compared to Sodium, Potassium, and Rubidium, which form both normal oxide and peroxide when they react with oxygen. Therefore, the correct answer is Lithium.

questions.text: What will be the product at the cathode during the electrolysis of brine in a Nelson's cell?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Brine is a highly concentrated solution of NaCl. Cathode is where reduction takes place and oxidation takes place on anode. In Brine there are two cations : H+ and Na+. Since hydrogen ion has a higher reduction potential (0.0V) than sodium ion (-2.71V), it will be reduced at cathode forming hydrogen gas.
Hydrogen ion has a higher reduction potential (0.0V) than sodium ion (-2.71V), it will be reduced at cathode forming hydrogen gas.
CO2 is not produced during Nelson's Cell reactions.
O2 is not produced during Nelson's Cell reactions.

questions.explanation: Brine is a highly concentrated solution of NaCl. Cathode is where reduction takes place and oxidation takes place on anode. In Brine there are two cations : H+ and Na+. Since hydrogen ion has a higher reduction potential (0.0V) than sodium ion (-2.71V), it will be reduced at cathode forming hydrogen gas.

questions.text: Li is different from its family members due to _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Lithium shows peculiar behaviour from its members. It has a small radius.
High charge density is one of the anomalous behaviour of Li.
The so-called anomalous properties of Li are due to the fact that Li is less electropositive than Na.

Li has a small radius and high charge density. It is also unexpectedly less electropositive than sodium. These characteristics of lithium are due to the fact that it only has 3 electrons in 2 shells. Therefore, A, B, and C are correct statements. This means the answer is D.

questions.explanation: Lithium is unique among alkali metals because it has a very small size, high charge density, and is less electropositive compared to other members of its group. These factors give it properties similar to magnesium (diagonal relationship). Hence, all the options together explain their differences.

questions.text: Melting and boiling points depend on_?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is correct because the number of electrons contributes to the strength of intermolecular forces, such as London dispersion forces, which increase with more electrons, leading to higher melting and boiling points.
Incorrect. The number of neutrons does not directly affect melting and boiling points as they do not participate in chemical bonding or influence intermolecular forces.

Incorrect. While paired electrons are stable, it is the unpaired electrons that significantly influence the strength of the forces between atoms or molecules, thereby affecting melting and boiling points.
Incorrect. The number of protons determines the atomic number and identity of an element, but does not directly influence the melting and boiling points of substances.

questions.explanation: Melting and boiling points generally increase with the number of electrons, because more electrons lead to stronger London dispersion (van der Waals) forces, resulting in higher melting/boiling points.

questions.text: Which of the following oxide is formed by alkali metals?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkali metals form basic oxides. When dissolved in water, they produce hydroxide ions and hydrogen gas giving basic solution. Alkali metals are basic in nature.
Acidic oxides are formed by non-metals and form acids in water.
Amphoteric oxides show both acidic and basic properties example: BeO ZnO etc.
This is incorrect information.

questions.explanation: Alkali metals are basic. The oxides they form are also basic due to this. They react strongly with water and create hydroxide ions and hydrogen gas, leaving a basic solution.

questions.text: Which one of the following substances conduct electricity by the movement of ions?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Graphite conducts electricity via the movement of electrons within its structure, not ions.
Copper is a metal that conducts electricity through the movement of electrons, not ions.
Molten sodium chloride conducts electricity through the movement of ions, making it an electrolyte.
Mercury is a metal that conducts electricity through electron movement, not ions.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is molten sodium chloride. When sodium chloride is in its molten state, it dissociates into sodium and chloride ions, which are free to move and conduct electricity. This is characteristic of electrolytes, which conduct electricity via ion movement. Graphite (A) and metals like Copper (B) and Mercury (D) conduct electricity through the movement of electrons, not ions, which is different from the mechanism in electrolytes.

questions.text: In Diaphragm Cell, level of brine in the anode compartment is kept slightly higher which prevents:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Hydroxide ions to reach the anode
options.explanation: Option A is correct as that makes sure that the flow of liquid is always from "left to right" - preventing any of the sodium hydroxide solutions formed from finding its way back to where chlorine is being produced. If Hydroxide ions reach anode, they will react explosively with Chloride ions.

This is incorrect because keeping the cell level of brine slightly higher in the anode compartment would not prevent chlorine gas from mixing. The level of brine has no impact on the mixing of chlorine gas.
This is incorrect because the cell level of brine in the anode compartment has no direct relation to the decay of the cathode. Cathode decay is not influenced by the level of brine.
This is incorrect because options B and C are individually incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct as that makes sure that the flow of liquid is always from "left to right" - preventing any of the sodium hydroxide solutions formed from finding its way back to where chlorine is being produced. If Hydroxide ions reach anode, they will react explosively with Chloride ions.

questions.text: The most abundant elements in earth's crust are _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Boron and Aluminum are not the most abundant elements in the Earth's crust. While they are present, they are not among the most abundant.
Calcium and Magnesium are important elements in the Earth's crust, but they are not the most abundant.

The nine most common elements in the Earth's crust are roughly 46% oxygen, 28% silicon, 8.3% aluminium, 6.9% iron, 2.4% calcium, 2.5% sodium, 2.4% magnesium, 2% potassium, and 0.61% titanium. Silicon and Aluminum are the two most abundant elements in the Earth's crust. Silicon is the most abundant element overall, making up about 28% of the Earth's crust by mass, while Aluminum is the third most abundant element, constituting around 8% of the crust.
"All" is not a correct answer in this context because it does not specify the elements. As discussed, Silicon and Aluminum are the correct choices.

questions.explanation: The nine most common elements in the Earth's crust are roughly 46% oxygen, 28% silicon, 8.3% aluminium, 6.9% iron, 2.4% calcium, 2.5% sodium, 2.4% magnesium, 2% potassium, and 0.61% titanium. Silicon and Aluminum are the two most abundant elements in the Earth's crust. Silicon is the most abundant element overall, making up about 28% of the Earth's crust by mass, while Aluminum is the third most abundant element, constituting around 8% of the crust.

questions.text: Which one of the following pairs show the diagonal relationship in the periodic table?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sodium (Na) and lithium (Li) do not show a diagonal relationship in the periodic table. While they are both alkali metals and share some similarities in their properties, they are not adjacent to each other in the periodic table and do not have the same electron configurations. Therefore, they do not exhibit the same kind of diagonal relationship as, for example, lithium and magnesium.

Lithium (Li) and magnesium (Mg) show a diagonal relationship in the periodic table. This is because they have similar ionic radii and electronegativities despite being in different groups. This similarity is because both elements have a filled 2s subshell and an empty 3p subshell. As a result, they exhibit similar chemical and physical properties, such as high reactivity and the ability to form stable compounds with other elements. This diagonal relationship is an example of how the periodic table can be used to predict the properties of elements based on their position in the table.

Lithium (Li) and beryllium (Be) do not show a diagonal relationship in the periodic table. While they are both in the same group (group 2) and share some similarities in their properties, they are not adjacent to each other in the periodic table and do not have the same electron configurations. Therefore, they do not exhibit the same kind of diagonal relationship as, for example, lithium and magnesium.

Boron (B) and beryllium (Be) do not show a diagonal relationship in the periodic table. While they are both in the same period (period 2) and share some similarities in their properties, they are not adjacent to each other in the periodic table and do not have the same electron configurations. Therefore, they do not exhibit the same kind of diagonal relationship.

questions.explanation: Lithium (Li) and magnesium (Mg) show a diagonal relationship in the periodic table. This is because they have similar ionic radii and electronegativities despite being in different groups. This similarity is due to the fact that both elements have a filled 2s subshell and an empty 3p subshell. As a result, they exhibit similar chemical and physical properties, such as high reactivity and the ability to form stable compounds with other elements. This diagonal relationship is an example of how the periodic table can be used to predict the properties of elements based on their position in the table.

questions.text: Lanthanides and Actinides resemble in:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Lanthanides and actinides do not resemble each other in terms of their ionization states. While both groups of elements can exhibit a wide range of oxidation states, the lanthanides generally only exhibit a +3 oxidation state, while the actinides can exhibit a wider range of oxidation states, including +3, +4, +5, +6 and even +7. This difference in oxidation states is due to the fact that the lanthanides have a more stable electron configuration, while the actinides have more flexibility in their electron configurations, allowing them to adopt a wider range of oxidation states.

Lanthanides and actinides do not resemble each other in terms of their oxidation states. While both groups of elements can exhibit a wide range of oxidation states, the lanthanides typically only exhibit a +3 oxidation state, while the actinides can exhibit a wider range of oxidation states, including +3, +4, +5, +6, and even +7. This difference in oxidation states is because the lanthanides have a more stable electron configuration, while the actinides have more flexibility in their electron configurations, allowing them to adopt a wider range of oxidation states.

Both lanthanides and actinides have similar electronic configurations. They both have their valence electrons in f-orbitals, which are shielded from the outer environment by the filled 5s and 6s orbitals. This gives them unique electronic and chemical properties, including their high electron density, magnetic and spectroscopic properties.
This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Both lanthanides and actinides have similar electronic configurations. They both have their valence electrons in f-orbitals, which are shielded from the outer environment by the filled 5s and 6s orbitals. This gives them unique electronic and chemical properties, including their high electron density, magnetic and spectroscopic properties.

questions.text: Isoelectronic species have same:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Isoelectronic species are atoms, ions, or molecules that have the same number of electrons and therefore the same electronic configuration. This means that they have the same number of electrons in their valence shell, which is the outermost shell of electrons that are involved in chemical bonding. Examples of isoelectronic species include O2-, F-, and Ne, which all have the same electronic configuration as neon (1s2 2s2 2p6)

Isoelectronic species do not necessarily have the same ionic size. Ionic size is determined by the number of protons in the nucleus and the number of electrons in the outermost shell of an ion. While isoelectronic species have the same number of electrons, they can have different numbers of protons, which can affect the size of the ion. For example, if two isoelectronic species have different numbers of protons, the ion with more protons will have a smaller ionic radius than the ion with fewer protons. Therefore, isoelectronic species can have different ionic radii, depending on the number of protons in the nucleus.

Isoelectronic species may not have identical reactivity. While they do have the same number of electrons, the number of protons in the nucleus can affect the overall charge of the ion, and therefore its chemical behavior. This can lead to differences in the reactivity of isoelectronic species. For example, two isoelectronic species with different numbers of protons may have different acidic or basic properties, or different abilities to form complexes with other molecules. Therefore, while isoelectronic species may have similar reactivity due to their identical electron configurations, they can also exhibit different chemical properties depending on the number of protons in their nuclei.

Isoelectronic species do not necessarily have the same pH. pH is a measure of the acidity or basicity of a solution and is determined by the concentration of hydrogen ions (H+) in the solution. While isoelectronic species have the same number of electrons, they can have different numbers of protons, which can affect their overall charge and therefore their acidity or basicity. For example, two isoelectronic species with different numbers of protons may have different acidic or basic properties, and therefore different pH values when dissolved in water. Therefore, isoelectronic species do not necessarily have the same pH.

questions.explanation: Isoelectronic species are atoms, ions, or molecules that have the same number of electrons and therefore the same electronic configuration. This means that they have the same number of electrons in their valence shell, which is the outermost shell of electrons that are involved in chemical bonding. Examples of isoelectronic species include O2-, F-, and Ne, which all have the same electronic configuration as neon (1s2 2s2 2p6).

questions.text: Which element is required for making Chlorophyll in Leaf?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Si (silicon) is beneficial for plants, as it can strengthen cell walls and help resist stress, but it is not a required component for making chlorophyll.
Ba (barium) is not an essential plant nutrient and is not involved in chlorophyll production.

Magnesium is required for the formation of chlorophyll in a leaf. Chlorophyll is an important component of the process of photosynthesis, which is the process by which light energy from the sun is converted to chemical energy to be utilized by the plants for growth.
Ca (calcium) is important for cell wall structure and nutrient uptake, but it is not the central element in the chlorophyll molecule.

questions.explanation: Magnesium (Mg) is the central atom in the chlorophyll molecule. It plays a key role in absorbing light energy during photosynthesis. Without Mg, plants cannot efficiently produce chlorophyll or perform photosynthesis.

questions.text: The first ionization potential of alkaline earth metal is greater than alkali metals because?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because ionization potential of metals doesn't depend upon reactivity.
This option is incorrect because alkaline earth metals don't have greater atomic radii.

This option is correct because the first ionization energies of alkaline earth metals are higher than those of the alkali metals. This is because of the increase in the nuclear charge of the alkaline earth metal.
This option is incorrect because option C is the correct option thus all cannot be the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Correct option is C. Alkaline earth metals have smaller atomic sizes as compared to alkali metals so electrons of outermost shells of alkaline earth metals are held tightly. As the nuclear charge increases it requires more energy to remove electrons(Ionization energy) hence ionization energy will be increased. The first ionization energies of alkaline earth metals are higher than those of the alkali metals. This is because of the increase in the nuclear charge of the alkaline earth metal.

questions.text: Metals are _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A substance that tends to bring about reduction by being oxidized and losing electrons is reducing agentMetals lose electrons when they react and make the other reactant gain electrons (reduction) hence they are "reducing agents".
A substance that tends to bring about oxidation by being reduced and gaining electrons is oxidizing agent
IncorrectOption B: A substance that tends to bring about oxidation by being reduced and gaining electrons is oxidizing agentNot the case with metals
A dehydrating agent is a substance that dries or removes water from a material. In chemical reactions where dehydration occurs, the reacting molecule loses a molecule of water.

questions.text: Melting points of Group II-A elements are higher than those of Group I-A because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Atoms of II-A elements have smaller size

reactivity is not a factor to be considered while talking about melting and boiling points.

options.text: Atoms of II-A elements provide two binding electrons
options.explanation: Metallic character as well as melting and boiling increase until period number three because the number of free electrons available for forming a metallic bond increases, so strong metallic bonds are formed As a result, melting Melting/Boiling boiling increases. Group-IIA offers two electrons for bonding while Group-IA offers one electron for bonding so it means IIA forms a stronger metallic bond.

Group-IIA offers two electrons for bonding while Group-IA offers one electron for bonding so it means IIA forms a stronger metallic bond.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct order of electronegativity (from highest to lowest)?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct order based on increasing electronegativity across the period. Fluorine is the most electronegative element, followed by Oxygen, then Nitrogen, and finally Carbon.
This order incorrectly places Nitrogen before Fluorine, which is not accurate since Fluorine is more electronegative.
This order incorrectly starts with Carbon as the most electronegative, which is incorrect. Carbon actually has the lowest electronegativity among these elements.
This order incorrectly places Carbon as more electronegative than both Fluorine and Oxygen, which is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Electronegativity is a measure of an atom's ability to attract and hold onto electrons. It generally increases across a period from left to right on the periodic table due to higher nuclear charge attracting electrons more strongly. Therefore, in the second period, Fluorine has the highest electronegativity, followed by Oxygen, Nitrogen, and Carbon. The correct order is F > O > N > C. Options B, C, and D are incorrect because they do not follow this established trend of increasing electronegativity.

questions.text: The flame colour of Ca in flame test is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Flame colors are produced from the movement of the electrons in the metal ions present in the compounds. Flame color of calcium is ORANGE RED. Hence, this option is correct.
Golden yellow is the flame color of sodium.
Red is the flame color of lithium.
Pink is the flame color of potassium.

questions.explanation: The flame of Ca is orange-red so, A is the correct option. A yellow flame is of Sodium (Na), Red flame is of Lithium (Li), and Pink flame is of Potassium (K) thus B, C, and D are incorrect.

questions.text: The chemical name of the baking soda is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sodium carbonate is known as "washing soda" and is primarily used in laundry detergents and water softeners. It is not used as a leavening agent in baking.
Sodium bicarbonate, also known as baking soda, is used in baking as a leavening agent. It reacts with acidic components to produce carbon dioxide gas, which helps dough rise.
Sodium chloride, commonly referred to as "table salt," is used for seasoning and preserving food. It does not serve the same chemical function as baking soda.
Sodium hydroxide, known as "caustic soda," is a strong base used in industrial cleaning and soap making. It is not safe for consumption and not related to baking soda.
This option is incorrect. Sodium bicarbonate and sodium chloride serve different purposes: sodium bicarbonate is a leavening agent, while sodium chloride is used for seasoning.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Sodium bicarbonate. Sodium bicarbonate, also known as baking soda, is a chemical compound used primarily as a leavening agent in baking. It reacts with acids to produce carbon dioxide gas, which causes dough to rise. The other options are incorrect: sodium carbonate is used in cleaning and water softening, sodium chloride is table salt, and sodium hydroxide is a caustic substance used in cleaning and manufacturing.

questions.text: The most dense element is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Lithium is a light metal with an atomic number of 3. It has a relatively low density, especially in comparison to some heavier elements. Its density is around 0.53 grams per cubic centimeter (g/cm³) at room temperature.
Potassium is an alkali metal with an atomic number of 19. It is a relatively light metal with a density of about 0.89 g/cm³ at room temperature.
Calcium is an alkaline earth metal with an atomic number of 20. It is denser than lithium and potassium. Calcium has a density of approximately 1.54 g/cm³ at room temperature.

The density (ratio between mass and volume as m/v) decreases from top to bottom in group 1(alkali metals) as the size increases and the nuclear charge decreases. In periods, density increases from left to right as atom size decreases and the nuclear charge increases. The alkaline earth metals are denser than the alkali metals due to their smaller size. The densities of alkaline earth metals do not show any regular trend with increasing atomic numbers. The density of these metal first decreases from Be to Ca and then increases from Ca to Ba. The trend of density amongst alkali metals: Li<K<Na<Rb<Cs The trend of density amongst alkaline earth metals: Ca<Mg<Be<Sr<Ba<Ra Hence, in this case, Ba is the densest element out of all the given options.
Atomic number: 37 Atomic mass: ~85.47 g/mol Rubidium is another alkali metal, and while it is heavier than lithium and potassium, it is still relatively light compared to some other elements.

questions.explanation: Down the group, the density of the elements increases, and Group II elements have a higher density than Group I elements because of their higher mass and lesser volumes. Ba is the only group II in all the options above.

questions.text: Which of the following elements has the highest boiling point?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Lithium (Li) has a boiling point of 1347 °C. Lithium is a group 1 (alkali metal) element and has a lower boiling point compared to beryllium.
Magnesium (Mg) has a boiling point of 1090 °C. Magnesium is a group 2 (alkaline earth metal) element and has a lower boiling point than beryllium.

Strontium (Sr) has a boiling point of 1382 °C. Strontium is also a group 2 element, and while it has a higher boiling point than lithium and magnesium, it still has a lower boiling point than beryllium.

Beryllium has the highest boiling point among the given options. Although it is a smaller atom with a lower atomic mass compared to strontium, beryllium has a unique electron configuration that contributes to stronger intermolecular forces. The boiling point of beryllium is about 2,471 degrees Celsius (4,480 degrees Fahrenheit).

questions.explanation: Group II elements have a higher boiling point than group I elements because of a higher charge, thus better attractive forces, so Li can’t be the answer given other group II elements. Down the group, the melting point and boiling point decreases so we can say that Be has the highest boiling point.
